betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Chicago Pile§f is the first nuclear reactor - a primitive early-game §bbreeder reactor§f based on the historical Chicago Pile-1. Its purpose is producing §bPlutonium§f from Uranium. §eNo GUI§f - all interactions occur directly with blocks.

§eConstruction:§f
• Build from §bGraphite Blocks§f (5x5 cubes or thin sheets)
• Use §bHand Drill§f to create holes for rods
• Insert §bUranium Rods§f (breeding material) and §bNeutron Source§f (e.g., Radium-Beryllium)
• §bAluminum Casings§f in empty holes provide cooling

§eBreeding Mechanics:§f
• Neutrons emit in 6 cardinal directions (max 5 block range)
• Uranium rods react when hit, producing heat and casting their own neutrons
• §aOptimal rod spacing: 1.5 blocks apart§f
• Heat causes thermal expansion, reducing efficiency up to 50%
• At §e50,000 depletion§f (75,000 in 528 mode), Uranium transmutes to §bPlutonium§f
• §bLithium rods§f can breed into §bTritium§f

§ePlutonium Extraction:§f
• Use §bScrewdriver§f to extract transmuted rods
• Process in §bTier 2+ Anvil§f for §bReactor Grade Plutonium§f

§c§4EXPLOSION WARNING:§f If any rod reaches §c1000 heat§f, the entire pile explodes.

§aTips:§f
• Use Hand Drill to check heat/depletion/flux levels
• Solid blocks (except graphite) block neutron rays - use for isolation
• Single Radium-Beryllium source can kickstart large arrays
• Use §bDesh-tier tools§f (high durability consumption)
